title: Configuration Management
description: Reading, writing, and validating Composer configuration (composer.json and global config)

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: User running Composer CLI commands to read/write/validate configuration

use_cases.name: Read Configuration Value
use_cases.description: Developer retrieves a specific config key value via `composer config [key]`
use_cases.triggering_method: ConfigCommand::execute() — no setting-value and no --unset; calls Config::all(), Config::getSourceOfValue(key)
use_cases.outcome: Displays resolved value with optional source info

use_cases.name: List All Configuration
use_cases.description: Developer lists entire configuration with `composer config --list`
use_cases.triggering_method: ConfigCommand::execute() with --list flag; calls Config::all(), Config::raw(), listConfiguration()
use_cases.outcome: Recursive key/value output of all config settings

use_cases.name: Set Configuration Value
use_cases.description: Developer writes a config key/value with `composer config [key] [value]`
use_cases.triggering_method: ConfigCommand::execute() — setting-value provided; calls handleSingleValue() or handleMultiValue() → configSource or authConfigSource write methods
use_cases.outcome: Value persisted to composer.json or config.json

use_cases.name: Unset Configuration Value
use_cases.description: Developer removes a config key with `composer config --unset [key]`
use_cases.outcome: Key removed from the JSON config file

use_cases.name: Edit Config in Editor
use_cases.description: Developer opens the config file in a text editor via `composer config --editor`
use_cases.triggering_method: ConfigCommand::execute() with --editor; resolves $EDITOR env var, opens configFile or authConfigFile path
use_cases.outcome: System editor launched with config file

use_cases.name: Manage Global Configuration
use_cases.description: Developer targets global config.json instead of local composer.json with --global flag
use_cases.outcome: All read/write/unset/list operations applied to global config

use_cases.name: Validate composer.json
use_cases.description: Developer validates a composer.json file with `composer validate [file]`
use_cases.triggering_method: ValidateCommand::execute() — creates ConfigValidator, calls validate(file, checkAll, checkVersion) returning [errors, publishErrors, warnings]; calls outputResult()

use_cases.name: Check Lock File Freshness
use_cases.description: Developer checks if composer.lock is up to date with `composer validate --check-lock`
use_cases.triggering_method: ValidateCommand::execute() — reads config.lock setting, calls Locker::isLocked(), Locker::isFresh(), Locker::getMissingRequirementInfo()
use_cases.outcome: Lock errors reported; included in exit code logic

use_cases.name: Validate Dependencies composer.json
use_cases.description: Developer validates composer.json of all installed dependencies with --with-dependencies
use_cases.triggering_method: ValidateCommand::execute() iterates RepositoryManager::getLocalRepository() packages, calls validator.validate() per package
use_cases.outcome: Aggregate exit code from all dependency validations

key_classes.name: ConfigCommand
key_methods: listConfiguration(contents, rawContents, output, k, showSource): void

key_classes.name: ValidateCommand
key_methods: outputResult(io, name, errors, warnings, checkPublish, publishErrors, checkLock, lockErrors, printSchemaUrl): void

key_classes.name: Config
key_classes.notable_behavior: get() checks COMPOSER_* env vars to override local config values; process() resolves {$refs} in config strings

key_relationships: BaseConfigCommand uses JsonFile (for reading/writing JSON config files)
BaseConfigCommand uses JsonConfigSource (implements ConfigSourceInterface, writes to JsonFile)
ValidateCommand uses ConfigValidator (for JSON schema validation)
ValidateCommand uses Locker (lock file freshness check)
Config reads COMPOSER_* environment variables to override settings
important_notes: ConfigCommand supports both project-local (composer.json) and global (config.json) config via --global
Auth credentials (github-oauth, http-basic, etc.) are stored in auth.json, not composer.json
Config::merge() handles special merging logic for oauth keys, allow-plugins, gitlab/github-domains, preferred-install, audit, filter
ValidateCommand dispatches PluginEvents::COMMAND after validation for plugin integration
Config::get() supports RELATIVE_PATHS flag to return relative instead of absolute paths
CONFIGURABLE_PACKAGE_PROPERTIES in ConfigCommand allows setting package metadata fields (name, type, description, etc.) directly
